calls.title: My MS_TEAMS meeting (ENEC QR-to-Survey)
calls.summary: Session covered QR-to-survey dashboard configuration and a critical bug preventing WO submission on low ratings. Fixes and configurations are targeted for end of week, after which the client will confirm UAT readiness.
key_points: Dashboard configurability and the need for admin training on customisation were primary discussion points.
Bug identified: work order submission fails on low ratings; 'thank you' page also missing — both to be fixed.
General comment field for all ratings will be implemented; offline feedback submission deemed out of scope.
Logo embedding in QR codes is not currently feasible but will be explored.
Preventing duplicate survey submissions faces technical constraints and is deprioritised.
Historical survey data onboarding ruled out due to absence of existing data.
decisions: Fixes and dashboard configurations to be rolled out by end of week.
UAT to commence upon email confirmation once fixes are live.
Real-time notifications for low-rated survey work orders will be implemented.

action_items.task: Fix work-order submission bug for low ratings and add 'thank you' page
Deliver admin training on dashboard customisation

risks: Bug in low-rating WO submission risks delaying UAT start; offline submission out of scope.
open_items: Email confirmation from client before UAT can begin; bug fix rollout by end of week.

calls.title: AlShalfa - Session 3
calls.summary: Requirements alignment session revealed the client's requested report template is an inspection checklist rather than a WO report. KPI formulas need correction by 13 May; requirement for point 25 needs client clarification.
key_points: Client-requested report format resembles an inspection checklist, not a standard work order report.
Aggregating data from many work orders into the requested format is technically impractical.
FCA report identified as a semi-annual inspection report — distinct from WO reporting.
KPI report formulas for hard services flagged for re-evaluation and correction.
Logo and QR code print sizing adjustments recommended to be tested before finalisation.
decisions: Clarification required on whether client needs a WO report or inspection report for point 25.
KPI report (hard services) to be updated by the following day.

action_items.task: Correct KPI report formulas for hard services
Clarify WO vs inspection report requirement for point 25
Test-print logo and QR code sizing before finalising

risks: KPI formula errors for hard services could affect reporting accuracy if not corrected promptly.
open_items: Client clarification on WO vs inspection report requirement; KPI formula corrections pending.

calls.title: Declined: Daily Facilio CAFM Workshops | Resolving Issues
calls.summary: Client management has directed that all dashboard functions move to the Yungo portal and purchase requests go through SAGE ERP. The team agreed on a reconfigured client portal layout focused on inventory and work order views.
key_points: Management directive to redirect all dashboard functionalities to Yungo portal — Facilio dashboards to be removed from client portal.
Purchase requests will be handled via SAGE ERP, not Facilio, to avoid redundant development.
PPM plans relocated to a work order view with list and calendar options, replacing the PPM calendar.
Inventory tracking to be presented as a list view under a new 'Inventory' folder (not as a dashboard).
Client requested spare parts vs. consumables distinction in inventory view.
Storekeepers will be granted stock-add access (not balance adjustment); training to be provided.
decisions: Remove Facilio client portal dashboards; redirect to Yungo portal.
Purchase requests handled via SAGE ERP — no Facilio development.
Create new 'Inventory' folder with Inventory Request and Item modules.
Assets displayed first in reconfigured client portal.

action_items.task: Reconfigure client portal navigation per agreed layout
Provide SAGE ERP access to relevant users
Create Inventory folder and configure spare parts/consumables views

risks: Removing Facilio dashboards from client portal in favour of Yungo could reduce Facilio stickiness; SAGE integration dependency.
open_items: SAGE ERP access provisioning; Yungo portal readiness.

calls.title: MODON - FM Oracle Integration Touch Points
calls.summary: Integration requirements for MODON FM Oracle procurement, inventory, and budget touchpoints were defined. All parties aligned on PR/PO flow, two-way inventory sync, and budget monitoring approach.
key_points: PRs initiated in Facilio for additional work orders will be pushed to Oracle for approval, with status updates flowing back.
Two-way inventory integration: Oracle provides item data; Facilio reports consumption.
Budget integration: Facilio will reflect Oracle's budget data and track spend against cost centres and projects.
Project codes, GL codes, stores, and vendor data must sync between Oracle and Facilio.
GRN initiation in Facilio will trigger Oracle updates for materials and services.
Real-time sync preferred for inventory and PR/PO status updates.
decisions: Specific use cases around budget consumption points to be refined and documented.
PR-to-Oracle flow confirmed for additional work order scenarios.

action_items.task: Refine and document budget consumption use cases
Define GL code and project code sync requirements

risks: Budget double-counting risk (PO vs GRN vs Invoice) flagged; service report push to Oracle for invoicing/GRN generation needs careful design.
open_items: Documentation of budget consumption use cases; GL code and project code availability from Oracle.

calls.title: ES Global UAT Demo
calls.summary: UAT demo covered most of the reactive flow but was blocked by a work permit functionality bug that prevented completion of the vendor process. A follow-up session is required before UAT can formally commence.
key_points: End-to-end reactive process demonstrated: service requests, work orders, inventory requests, quotations, and work permits.
Dashboard, portfolio, and asset management features showcased.
Storekeeper (not technician) identified as correct role for material requests — process re-explained.
Quotation stage needed for in-house work orders (similar to vendor flow) — to be addressed.
Before/after photo attachments for work orders desired as mandatory — pending feature release.
Critical bug: work permit functionality broken, preventing continuation of vendor process.
decisions: Work permit bug and in-house quotation requirement to be addressed in a follow-up session.
UAT user access to be provided (initially admin) to a list of users.

action_items.task: Fix work permit functionality bug
Arrange follow-up session to complete vendor process demo and address in-house quotation
Provision UAT user access for listed users

risks: Work permit bug is a UAT blocker; in-house quotation flow missing; mandatory photo attachment feature not yet released.
open_items: Work permit bug fix; follow-up session scheduling; mandatory photo feature release.

calls.title: MODON - AM Oracle Integration Touch Points
calls.summary: Asset Management Oracle integration touchpoints for MODON were scoped: PR/PO, contracts, vendors, inventory, warranty, and RFQ flows were defined with Oracle as source of truth for procurement.
key_points: Oracle as source of truth for PR creation; PO status updates flow back to Facilio.
Vendor, contract, and PO details including attachments to sync from Oracle to Facilio.
Cost centre and project information sync from Oracle to Facilio — confirmation and integration to be validated.
Contract variations: PR initiated in Facilio → new PO/contract in Oracle → syncs back.
Performance bonds and insurance data: manual input preferred for existing contracts; Oracle sync for future.
Warranty claims managed in Oracle, pushed to Facilio — bidirectional integration avoided to prevent duplication.
RFQ process: Facilio → Far Vision approval → Oracle for sourcing.
Budget management remains manual in Facilio; auto-reduces on work completion; no Oracle budget sync.
decisions: Oracle is the source of truth for PR; Facilio initiates and reflects status.
Bidirectional warranty integration rejected to avoid duplication.
Inventory managed primarily in Facilio by FMSP; future bidirectional sync for quantity/stocking to be assessed.

action_items.task: Confirm cost centre and project code availability and integration feasibility
Document contract variation and RFQ integration flows

risks: Cost centre/project sync availability not yet confirmed; manual budget management in Facilio creates reconciliation risk.
open_items: Cost centre/project data availability confirmation from Oracle; Far Vision approval integration design.

calls.title: ENEC - BRD - Scope Alignment: Office Space Management
calls.summary: BRD scope alignment for ENEC Workspace Management. Phase 1 will integrate HR and Outlook; real-time occupancy via a 'brain' system is deferred. Key requirements documented for desk allocation and space analytics.
key_points: HR system integration is a primary requirement for onboarding, offboarding, and leave management.
Date-range specific desk assignments and deallocations required for temporary vacancy management.
Analytics on space utilisation, occupancy, and availability are critical for decision-making.
Outlook integration desired for meeting room bookings to provide unified view and analytics.
Real-time occupancy from a 'brain' system identified as a potential future enhancement.
Need to handle permanent vs. temporary desk allocations with clear business rules.
Phase 1 to focus on HR and Outlook integrations; other functionalities in future phases.
decisions: Phase 1 scoped to HR and Outlook core integrations.
Real-time occupancy 'brain' system deferred to a future phase.

action_items.task: Document scope and integration requirements in BRD
Define business rules for permanent vs. temporary desk allocation

risks: Reliance on multiple systems (HR, Outlook, 'brain') creates integration complexity; temporary allocation logic requires careful business rule design.
open_items: HR system details and API availability; Outlook integration feasibility confirmation.

calls.title: CWRC Oracle Integration Requirement
calls.summary: Oracle integration requirements for CWRC were defined covering MR/PR automation, budget visibility, GL codes, and KPI-based invoice validation. Finance and Procurement teams need to be involved in subsequent meetings.
key_points: MR approval in Facilio to auto-trigger PR creation in Oracle; status feedback back to Facilio.
Work order costs (labour and materials) managed in Facilio; material costs integrated into Oracle.
Oracle is source of truth for budget setting; Facilio displays budget info.
GL codes from Oracle essential for expense mapping.
Facilio to generate KPI reports for Oracle to validate contractor invoice deductions.
Contract creation flow: Facilio → Oracle for invoicing.
Finance and Procurement teams flagged for inclusion in upcoming Oracle meetings.
decisions: Oracle remains source of truth for budget; Facilio is read-only for budget visibility.
Inventory integration between Oracle and Facilio is a shared requirement.

action_items.task: Arrange next meeting with CWRC Finance and Procurement teams and Oracle
Document GL code mapping requirements

risks: Finance and Procurement teams not yet engaged — their absence could cause rework; GL code mapping complexity.
open_items: Finance/Procurement team availability; Oracle GL code provision; KPI report template definition.

calls.title: [THCE <> Facilio] : Moon Flower Oracle Integration Touch Points
calls.summary: Brief touchpoints session for THCE Moon Flower Oracle integration. Requirements follow the MODON Oracle integration pattern; documentation to be completed.
key_points: Oracle integration touchpoints for Moon Flower project discussed.
Consistent with MODON Oracle integration pattern — procurement, inventory, and budget flows.
decisions: Integration requirements to be documented aligned with MODON approach.

action_items.task: Document Moon Flower Oracle integration requirements

open_items: Oracle integration documentation from MODON to serve as reference.

calls.title: Facilio Implementation Update (Teyseer)
calls.summary: UAT preparation update for Teyseer. Data quality issues with duplicate job plans will be addressed post-import by supervisors. UAT is on track for week of 19 May; functional test report is due 18 May.
key_points: Duplicate and inconsistent job plans for CTGV and CTGV ACC — tasks and checklists misaligned.
Decision to import data as-is; supervisors to review and correct job plans post-import.
Rough work order count from IBM Maximo still needed to validate exported data.
UAT scheduled for week of 19 May; functional test report due 18 May.
Team confirmed on track for UAT handover despite potential holiday impacts.
Project plan status needs accurate module-level updates for management reporting.
Vendor and contract data access from client to be provided for verification.
decisions: Import job plan data as-is; post-import corrections by supervisors.
UAT week of 19 May confirmed; functional test report due 18 May.

action_items.task: Obtain rough WO count from IBM Maximo for data validation
Submit functional test report
Update project plan status by module for management reporting
Provide vendor and contract data access for verification

risks: Duplicate job plans and missing IBM Maximo WO count create data quality risk; holiday impacts flagged but managed.
open_items: IBM Maximo WO count; Teyseer vendor/contract data access; functional test report by 18 May.

calls.title: Butlins <> Facilio - Kick Off
calls.summary: Successful kickoff meeting for Butlins implementation. Phase 1 (reactive maintenance) targets June 15 go-live. A requirements workshop is scheduled for next week with pre-workshop prep session.
key_points: Project team, methodology, and initial scope established.
Phase 1: portfolio, service requests, and reactive maintenance — targeting June 15 go-live.
Phase 2: cleaning and reservation processes; technical integration discussions planned.
Key risks identified: stakeholder availability, scope creep, data onboarding challenges.
Workshop scheduled for following week to finalise requirements and create BRDs.
30-minute pre-workshop session planned to review onboarding template and identify pain points.
decisions: Phase 1 reactive maintenance go-live target: June 15, 2026.
Workshop next week for requirements finalisation and BRD creation.

action_items.task: Prepare onboarding template and share before pre-workshop session
Conduct pre-workshop 30-min session to identify pain points
Finalise BRDs at workshop

risks: Stakeholder availability, scope creep, and data onboarding challenges flagged as key project risks.
open_items: Workshop attendance and BRD sign-off from Butlins stakeholders; Jake Lowe in-person attendance confirmed.

calls.title: RE: NetSuite Integration - Status Check (Deyaar DCM)
calls.summary: NetSuite integration status check for Deyaar DCM. Site creation and vendor sync are live, but PR/PO integrations are failing and project sign-off is blocked. Key person Lina is unavailable, creating a scope gap that both sides need to resolve.
key_points: Integration sign-off goal: confirm all modules (site creation, vendor, service requests, PR/PO) working in production.
Site creation and vendor data sync confirmed active in production.
PR and PO integrations reportedly failing — APIs reportedly exist but failing.
Key integration owner Lina is unavailable, creating documentation and scope gaps.
NetSuite consultant stated PR/PO were not explicitly assigned tasks.
Both parties agreed to investigate APIs and codebases independently.
decisions: NetSuite consultant to receive specific requirements by email to proceed with fixes.
Both sides to investigate existing APIs to identify and resolve failures.

action_items.task: Send specific PR/PO integration requirements to NetSuite consultant by email
Investigate failing PR/PO APIs in Facilio codebase
Investigate failing APIs on NetSuite side once requirements received

risks: Absence of key integration owner Lina; lack of documentation; unclear scope attribution for PR/PO integration.
open_items: Lina's availability or knowledge transfer; NetSuite consultant receiving requirements email; API investigation outcomes.

calls.title: MODON - Moon Flower Oracle Integration Touch Points
calls.summary: MODON Moon Flower Oracle integration touchpoints finalised including MR→PR automation, dual-store inventory sync, GRN attachment, invoice capture, and soft budget monitoring. Invoice integration was a new touchpoint added in this session.
key_points: Approved MRs in Facilio automatically trigger PR creation in Oracle.
Dual-store approach (red and green zones) in Facilio confirmed; data sync from Oracle's centralised store.
GRN process executed in Oracle; approved GRNs attached to MRs in Facilio.
New touchpoint: invoices generated in Oracle captured in Facilio for tracking and GRN/SCS process.
Budget integration into Facilio for monitoring and notifications — not for hard blocking expenditure.
Work completion reports can be generated from Facilio work orders with custom templates.
decisions: MR approval triggers automatic PR in Oracle — confirmed.
Invoice touchpoint added: Oracle invoices captured in Facilio for tracking.
Budget integration for soft monitoring only (no hard block).

action_items.task: Document Moon Flower Oracle integration touchpoints formally
Design work completion report custom template for SCS process

risks: Budget monitoring only (no hard block) may allow overspend; GRN/SCS process relies on Oracle invoice accuracy.
open_items: Oracle invoice API availability; GRN sync design; custom report template definition.

calls.title: 1st Contract <> Facilio - Data Onboarding & Workshop Plan
calls.summary: Pre-workshop alignment for 1st Contract configuration. The full-day workshop is confirmed for 18 May. Most data is ready; quote/invoice templates are outstanding from the client.
key_points: Full-day workshop scheduled for 18th to configure Facilio system.
Portfolio hierarchy to be structured at site level, associated with clients.
Service request categories, priorities, and SLA matrix largely prepared by Stacey Buckley.
RAMs configuration (generic vs. dynamic) deferred to workshop.
Vendor onboarding nearing completion; details to be shared when ready.
Quote and invoice templates need example formats from 1st Contract for customisation.
Additional exported data fields can be accommodated via extra Facilio site attributes.
Primary service: building fabric maintenance.
decisions: Workshop scheduled for 18 May for system configuration.
RAMs configuration to be detailed at the workshop.
Jake Lowe to attend in person at client office for workshop.

action_items.task: Share quote and invoice template examples before workshop
Conduct workshop and configure Facilio system
Complete vendor onboarding and share details

risks: RAMs configuration complexity to be resolved at workshop; vendor onboarding slightly pending.
open_items: Client-provided quote and invoice templates; vendor contact details; Jake Lowe's in-person attendance.

calls.title: Houchens Weekly Touchbase
calls.summary: Weekly touchbase for Houchens implementation. Vendor onboarding is the key concern — a master plan and calendar are urgently needed. Go-live remains tentatively end of May with a six-week hyper-care period planned.
key_points: Dashboard visibility and arrangement on mobile to be optimised.
Site and asset decommissioning process clarified: retirement then decommission (two-step).
Workaround for mandatory inspection limitation: manual entry for overdue items; product fix pending.
Vendor onboarding progressing slowly — master plan and training calendar needed.
QR code generation and bulk printing process for asset identification explained.
Go-live tentatively set for end of month; six-week hyper-care period to follow.
374 active sites to be validated against contract costs.
decisions: Build master plan and calendar for vendor onboarding and training.
Go-live tentatively end of May 2026.
Validate active site count (374) against contract.

action_items.task: Create master plan and calendar for vendor onboarding and training
Validate 374 active sites against contract costs
Deliver product fix for mandatory inspection limitation

risks: Vendor onboarding slow; mandatory inspection workaround needed; site count validation pending.
open_items: Vendor onboarding master plan; mandatory inspection product fix; site count validation.

calls.title: Facilio <> HGFS Weekly Project Call
calls.summary: Weekly project call with HGFS focused on platform enhancements: admin delete for WOs, integrated proposal/invoicing, FSM module introduction, and offline inspections. Client is actively deepening their platform usage.
key_points: Delete access for work orders to be enabled for admins to manage duplicates.
Proposal and invoice generation within Facilio to be integrated — reduces reliance on Excel.
Custom email domain for branded communication possible; requires commercial discussion.
Quotation module supports itemised pricing and pre-defined service rates.
FSM (Field Service Management) module introduced for scheduling and dispatching.
Offline mode for inspections now available on mobile app.
Inspections can be automated and triggered by PPM schedules.
Work order comment formatting issue (copy-paste related) being investigated.
decisions: Enable admin delete access for work orders.
Explore custom email domain integration commercially.

action_items.task: Enable delete access for work orders for HGFS admins
Initiate commercial discussion for custom email domain
Investigate and resolve work order comment formatting issue

risks: Custom email domain requires commercial agreement; comment formatting bug could affect client communications.
open_items: Commercial approval for custom email domain; WO comment formatting bug resolution.
